name: Capsaicin
summary: Capsaicin is a capsaicinoid associated with chili peppers. Evidence level: moderate.
description: Capsaicin activates TRPV1, driving afferent signaling that can alter pain perception and energy-related pathways. Human support is strongest for TRPV1 effects.
safetyNotes: Burning, erythema, and local irritation common; avoid eyes, mucosa, and broken skin.

claimMap.claim: v8.2: Topical capsaicin appears effective for osteoarthritis pain, with concentrations generally 0.025–0.075% in trials.
claimMap.notes: v8.2: Topical capsaicin appears effective for osteoarthritis pain, with concentrations generally 0.025–0.075% in trials.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: topical 0.0125–0.075% capsaicin used in OA trials; oral supplement claims not supported
